title: Sweet Smoky Algerian Pepper Tagine
description: A tangy and gooey kid-friendly Algerian dish featuring roasted peppers, this tagine is perfect for a weeknight dinner, and its ayurvedic tamasic friendly properties make it a great option for a comforting meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place the bell peppers on a baking sheet and roast for 30 minutes.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool.
Peel the peppers and chop them into large pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a large stone bowl or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the roasted peppers, chicken broth, and honey to the bowl.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
